text:
BOTTOM LINE — Tech wreck, defensives quietly bid. Risk-off skew was extreme with SOXX -7.88% and XLK -4.14% as hawkish Fed expectations hit AI hardware and high-duration growth while staples and defensives drew capital rotation. Semis-led liquidation (SMH -7.01%, DRAM -14.25%) and single-name hits like KLAC -9.17% and MU -12.11% reflected crowded positioning unwinds rather than broad macro stress, with bonds, credit, and dollar moves remaining orderly.

INDICES — VIX 19.5, up vs its 20d MA 17.6, confirming vol bid. By 1d return: DIA -0.09%, RSP -0.34%, IJR -0.34%, IWM -0.96%, IJH -1.01%, SPY -1.45%, GLD -1.89%, QQQ -3.29%, SLV -5.40%, SOXX -7.88%. STYLE: SMALL > LARGE by 1.11pp (small -0.34%, large -1.45%). Most notable cross-asset move was SLV -5.40% in tandem with GLD -1.89%, signaling a sharp precious-metals de-risking.

VOLUME — QQQ 1.4x, IWM 1.2x, and RSP 1.1x flagged indices under active scrutiny while IJH 0.7x and IJR 0.6x were comparatively sidelined. SPY volume ratio is implicitly near the 1.03x indices average, while QQQ 1.4x with -3.29% is classic distribution, and GLD 0.9x with -1.89% plus XLV/XLRE/XLV 0.8x suggests a low-conviction defensive bid into the tech drawdown.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLP Staples +1.87%, XLRE Real Estate +1.41%, XLV Healthcare +1.41%, XLU Utilities +0.78%, XLE Energy +0.74%, XLC Comms +0.38%, XLF Financials +0.34%, XLY Discretionary -1.03%, XLB Materials -1.45%, XLI Industrials -2.01%, XLK Technology -4.14%. Top industries: KIE +2.35%, REZ +1.85%, PPH +1.79%, IHI +1.61%; bottom: SILJ -5.76%, SMH -7.01%, SOXX -7.88%, DRAM -14.25%. Money-in flowed to XLP, XLV, and KIE/PPH quality-defensive baskets, while money-out was concentrated in XLK, SMH, SOXX, and DRAM as rate-hike fears repriced AI hardware. Rotation-state label: defensive risk-off.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR_vol 0.85 with skew +10.8% and VIX 19.5 just above its 20d MA 17.6 shows modest protection demand but not panic. QQQ PCR_vol 1.54 and skew +12.1% alongside VXN 32.4 over its 20d MA 26.6 and KLAC 3.3x vol with -9.17% point to concentrated fear and hedging in tech. MOVE 68.8 below its 20d MA 72.0 indicates bond vol stress is contained, so equity turbulence is primarily valuation and Fed-path driven rather than cross-asset chaos.

CROSS-ASSET — Dollar strength via UUP +0.32% plus SLV -5.40% and GLD -1.89% highlighted a classic higher-rate, stronger-USD pressure on real assets even as TLT +0.13% and IEF +0.13% stayed calm. USO -1.27% with OVX 46.6 below its 20d MA 56.5 shows ongoing energy weakness without an accompanying vol shock, underlining that today’s stress was tech-specific rather than broad commodity capitulation.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed 58.9/100 sat neutral while MU -12.11% (semis), VRT +4.87% (AI infrastructure), and MRVL +13% (AI networking) were standout single-name movers. Sector momentum extremes were XLK -4.14% on the downside and XLP +1.87% on the upside, with DRAM -14.25% and KIE +2.35% anchoring basket momentum tails. AIQ 4.2x vol with -5.57% and KLAC 3.3x vol with -9.17% both flagged distribution in AI-linked hardware rather than quiet profit-taking. Watch tomorrow: VRT +4.87% for resilience amid sector stress and potential AI infrastructure leadership; GLD -1.89% for continuation risk given GVZ 27.4 above its 20d MA 26.5; XLI -2.01% for possible follow-through if cyclical weakness broadens beyond tech.
